title: cornus alba
intro: Cornus alba, also known as the Red Osier Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to Asia and Europe, characterized by its bright red stems and clusters of white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and fight off infections. The health benefits of Cornus alba include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Cornus alba can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
